hypothesis: Contrastive multimodal pretraining with SMILES anchors will induce emergent SO(3) invariance in 3D molecular encodings only when the symbolic modality is inherently invariant to the transformation; consequently, training on conformer-variant SMILES representations (e.g., 3D coordinate-dependent SMILES variants or augmented SMILES with stereochemical descriptors) will degrade rotational invariance in the learned 3D embeddings proportionally to the variance introduced in the symbolic modality.
